use_case_name: Authorization at EVSE using ISO 15118 External Identification Means (EIM)

structured.name: Authorization at EVSE using ISO 15118 External Identification Means (EIM)
structured.objective:
To authorize the EV via the Charging Station, with help of the CSMS. Also see ISO15118-1, use
case Objective D4, page 28.
structured.description:
The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest message based on information provided by the
EV. Also see ISO15118-1, use case Description D4 up to and including "NOTE", page 28.
scenario:
15118
See ISO15118-1, use case Description (Scenarion Description) D4, page

1. The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest with an idToken containing the External
Identification Means (EIM).
2. The CSMS responds with an AuthorizeResponse.
structured.prerequisites: Communication between EV and EVSE SHALL be established successfully.
structured.postconditions: Authorization is successful. Also see ISO15118-1, use case End conditions D4, page 28.
structured.remarks:
Please note that all identification means mentioned in the previous section can be applied to this
use case. The only difference is the availability of 15118 communication.

requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send the identification to the CSMS
for validation.

EV Driver SHALL activate the authorization within a specific time
after connecting the EV to the EVSE or the EVSE SHALL have an
HMI to authorize the restart of the identification process.

chunks.content:
# C08 - Authorization at EVSE using ISO 15118 External Identification Means (EIM)

**Objective:** To authorize the EV via the Charging Station, with help of the CSMS. Also see ISO15118-1, use
case Objective D4, page 28.

**Description:** The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest message based on information provided by the
EV. Also see ISO15118-1, use case Description D4 up to and including "NOTE", page 28.

**Actors:** EV, Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
15118
See ISO15118-1, use case Description (Scenarion Description) D4, page
8.
OCPP
1. The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest with an idToken containing the External
Identification Means (EIM).
2. The CSMS responds with an AuthorizeResponse.

**Prerequisites:** Communication between EV and EVSE SHALL be established successfully.

**Postconditions:** Authorization is successful. Also see ISO15118-1, use case End conditions D4, page 28.

**Remarks:** Please note that all identification means mentioned in the previous section can be applied to this
use case. The only difference is the availability of 15118 communication.

metadata.heading: C08 - Authorization at EVSE using ISO 15118 External Identification Means (EIM)
metadata.use_case_name: Authorization at EVSE using ISO 15118 External Identification Means (EIM)

chunks.content:
# C08 - Requirements

**C08.FR.01**
The Charging Station SHALL send the identification to the CSMS
for validation.

**C08.FR.02**
EV Driver SHALL activate the authorization within a specific time
after connecting the EV to the EVSE or the EVSE SHALL have an
HMI to authorize the restart of the identification process.
